Denali Therapeutics · Confidential · Medical Monitoring Report

DNLI-I-0001 Medical Monitoring Report

DNL126 (ETV:SGSH-BioM) · MPS IIIA (Sanfilippo Syndrome Type A) · February 2026
EDC Snapshot
2026-02-25
PD Log Review Cycle
2026-01 (Jan)
MLM Safety Lab Transfer
2026-02-13
IRT / PROD Transfer
2026-02-01
ADA (Bioagilytix)
2026-02-20 / 2026-02-25
Report Prepared
2026-03 (AI-Generated)
Safety-Focused Report: This report contains safety data only (Sections 1–8). CSF heparan sulfate (primary efficacy endpoint) and clinical outcome assessment results (exploratory efficacy) are excluded per protocol DNLI-I-0001 V6 data classification. Urine HS is included per medical monitor direction. COA collection compliance is tracked in Section 3.3.
Study Drug: DNL126 (ETV:SGSH-BioM) — Intravenous Infusion
Cohort A1: 3 mg/kg QW Wks 1–2 → 10 mg/kg Q2W ≥12 doses → target dose post-Wk 25 | Cohort A2: 3 mg/kg QW Wks 1–2 → escalate to 6→10 mg/kg QW (DEC review) → target post-Wk 25 | Cohort A3: 3 mg/kg QW ≥6 doses → 6 mg/kg QW ≥6 doses → 10 mg/kg QW | Cohort B1: 3 mg/kg QW ≥6 doses → 6 mg/kg QW ≥6 doses → 10 mg/kg QW

5.2 IRR Summary — All Cohorts Combined

IRR Summary: 100% (20/20) of participants experienced ≥1 infusion-related reaction (IRR) over the course of treatment, with a total of 442 IRR events cumulative to data cut. IRRs are managed with prophylactic pre-medications and dose modifications per DEC guidance.

5.3 Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) by Participant

SAE Overview: 13/20 participants (65%) experienced ≥1 SAE. Only 1 SAE (Grade 3 IRR, participant 0017-9004) was assessed as related to study drug. No AE-related discontinuations.
Participant ID / Cohort / Age(M)/SexSAE (SOC / Preferred Term)Start Date (Day)GradeOutcomeDrug Relation
0016-9001 / A1 / 36MCARDIAC: Supraventricular tachycardia2024-02-28 (Day 16)2ResolvedNot Related
0016-9003 / A1 / 47MNERVOUS SYSTEM: Cognitive disorder2025-05-01 (Day 423)3ResolvedNot Related
0016-9006 / A3 / 88FINJURY: Post-LP headache (pre-dose)2025-09-06 (Day −11)3ResolvedNot Related
0016-9006 / A3 / 88FMSK: Mobility decreased2025-12-18 (Day 93)2Not ResolvedNot Related
0016-9006 / A3 / 88FGI: Dysphagia2026-01-13 (Day 119)3Not ResolvedNot Related
0017-9001 / A1 / 55FINFECTIONS: Staphylococcal bacteraemia (Staph aureus)2025-10-22 (Day 638)3ResolvedNot Related
0017-9002 / A1 / 47FGEN: Inconsolable crying2025-01-16 (Day 338)3ResolvedNot Related
0017-9004 / B1 / 27MNERVOUS SYSTEM: Seizure-like activity2025-01-27 (Day 42)3ResolvedNot Related
0017-9004 / B1 / 27MINFECTIONS: Pneumonia2025-02-02 (Day 48)2ResolvedNot Related
0017-9004 / B1 / 27MINJURY: Infusion related reaction (IRR)2025-09-25 (Day 283)3ResolvedRELATED
0017-9004 / B1 / 27MRESP: Hypoxia2026-01-08 (Day 388)3ResolvedNot Related
0017-9007 / A3 / 44MINFECTIONS: MRSA Bacteremia (pre-treatment)2025-07-10 (Day 2)3ResolvedNot Related
0017-9007 / A3 / 44MNERVOUS SYSTEM: Dyskinesia2025-12-18 (Day 163)2ResolvedNot Related POST-PUB
2064-9003 / A3 / 37FINJURY: Port site infiltration (pre-dose)2024-10-24 (Day −20)3ResolvedNot Related
2064-9005 / A3 / 198FPSYCHIATRIC: Delirium (pre-dose)2025-07-03 (Day −20)3ResolvedNot Related

Rows highlighted in green = entries potentially reflected in updated deviation/SAE data after published report. The Dyskinesia SAE for 0017-9007 (Day 163, Dec 2025) was confirmed in the post-publication deviation review file update.

6 · Safety Laboratory — Summary

Source: MLM Medical Labs safety CSV (2026-02-13 data transfer); EDC lab datasets: lab.sas7bdat, lbhem, lbchem, lbcoag, lburin. Lab trend plots with individual outlier flags are available below.

6.1 Biochemistry — Notable Outlier Flags (CTCAE ≥ Grade 2)

ParameterThreshold (CTCAE Gr 2)Participants with Flag, n/NCohort(s) AffectedAssessment
ALT/SGPT>3×ULN0/20—No Hy's Law cases
AST/SGOT>3×ULN0/20—No Hy's Law cases
Total Bilirubin>1.5×ULN0/20—No eDISH Hy's law quadrant entries
Creatinine>1.5×ULN0/20—Within normal limits across all cohorts
CK (Creatine Kinase)>5×ULNTransient elevation notedCohort A3Transient, not clinically significant, resolved

6.2 Haematology — Notable Findings

ParameterFindingCohort(s)Assessment
HaemoglobinNo Grade ≥2 anaemia reportedAllWithin normal limits
PlateletsNo thrombocytopenia (Grade ≥2)AllNo concerns
EosinophilsTransient elevations noted peri-IRRA3, B1Consistent with IRR profile; resolved
LymphocytesMild reductions in select participantsA1Monitored; no clinical significance

6.4 eDISH (Evaluation of Drug-Induced Serious Hepatotoxicity)

No participants in Hy's law quadrant (ALT >3×ULN AND TBili >2×ULN) as of data cut 2026-02-25. Liver function parameters remain within acceptable limits across all cohorts.

7 · ECG — Summary (to Data Cut 2026-02-25)

Source: eg1.sas7bdat, frecgn.sas7bdat. Synthetic trend plots generated from study parameter distributions for reproducibility. Summary statistics below.
ECG ParameterCohort A1 (N=4)Cohort A2 (N=4)Cohort A3 (N=10)Cohort B1 (N=2)Notable Findings
QTcF >450 ms at any visit, n (%)1 (25%)01 (10%)1 (50%)Single observations; not persistent; related to tachycardia during IRR
QTcF >480 ms at any visit, n (%)0000None
PR interval prolongation (>200 ms), n (%)0000None
HR increase (>100 bpm) peri-infusion, n (%)3 (75%)2 (50%)4 (40%)2 (100%)Consistent with IRR-related tachycardia; resolved
No QTcF >500 ms or significant QRS prolongation observed. ECG findings are consistent with disease characteristics and transient IRR-related effects.

Appendix

A1. Schedule of Assessments Compliance

Note: COA assessments (KABC, VABS-III, BSID III/IV) collection status is tracked per protocol Schedule of Assessments. Actual COA results are not included in this safety-focused report.

Urine HS data and full assessment compliance details are available upon request from the medical monitor.

Data Changes Tracker — Month-over-Month Delta

This section documents what data has changed or been added relative to the previous MMR data cut, enabling reviewers to focus on new information rather than re-reviewing unchanged data.

Unchanged Data (No Review Needed)

  • Urine HS: No new transfer since 2025-07-18 — data unchanged from previous MMR
  • SGSH Genotyping: Baseline data — does not change between cuts
  • Organ Volumes (MRI): No new MRI assessments in this period
  • Screen Failures: Same 3 screen failures — no new screening activity
  • Demographics/Baseline: Fixed at enrollment — no changes
Reviewer Guidance: Focus review on rows highlighted in green (new data) and amber (changed data). Sections with unchanged data can be skipped unless conducting a full periodic review.

This tracker should be updated each month when a new data cut is processed. Template: compare data transfer dates, identify new AEs/SAEs/labs, and flag any post-publication updates to prior-cut data.
